zh.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman是John Freeman的女兒，於1595年出生在英國諾福爾可維蒙達哈姆。她於1615年與Henry Chamberlin Sr在英國諾福爾可辛加姆結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1679年1月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼赫爾享年84歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼赫爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman是John Freeman的女儿，于1595年出生在英国諾福爾可維蒙達哈姆。她于1615年与Henry Chamberlin Sr在英国諾福爾可辛加姆结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1679年1月28日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼赫爾享年84岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼赫爾。

nameMeanings.name: Freeman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English freman, fremon ‘freeman, free-born man’ (Old English frēomann, frīgmann), used also occasionally as a personal name. As an African American surname it was in many cases adopted as a name denoting a man freed of slavery. See also Fryman and Free .
Irish: Anglicized (‘translated’) form of Gaelic Ó Saoraidhe (see Seery ).

variants.name: Free
meanings: English: nickname or status name from Middle English fre ‘free’ (Old English frēo), with reference to either tenurial status (‘free born’) or to character (‘noble, generous, open-handed’).
Americanized form of Swiss German and German   Frei   and   Frey  .

variants.name: Fryman
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German Frymann or German Freimann (see Freiman ), and possibly also of the Dutch cognate Vrijman.
English: from Middle English friman ‘free-born man’ (Old English frīomann, frīgmann), symonymous with Freeman 1.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman was born in 1595, in Wymondham, Norfolk, England as the daughter of John Freeman. She married Henry Chamberlin Sr in 1615, in Hingham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 28 January 1679, in Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 84, and was buried in Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman est née en 1595, à Wymondham, Norfolk, Angleterre, fille de John Freeman. Elle a épousé Henry Chamberlin Sr en 1615, à Hingham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 janvier 1679, à Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman wurde 1595 in Wymondham, Norfolk, England, als Tochter von John Freeman geboren. Sie hat 1615 in Hingham, Norfolk, England Henry Chamberlin Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Januar 1679 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman è nata il nel 1595 nel Wymondham, Norfolk, England come figlia di John Freeman. Lei sposato Henry Chamberlin Sr nel 1615, nel Hingham, Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 28 gennaio 1679, nel Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freemanは1595年にイギリスノーフォルクウイモンドハムでJohn Freemanの娘として生まれた。1615年にイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムでHenry Chamberlin Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1679年1月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハルで84歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman(은)는 1595년 영국 노르폴크 윈이몽함에서 John Freeman의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1615년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 Henry Chamberlin Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1679년 1월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 훌에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 훌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman nasceu em 1595, em Wymondham, Norfolk, Inglaterra filha de John Freeman. Ela casou-se com Henry Chamberlin Sr em 1615, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de janeiro de 1679, em Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman родилась в 1595 г. в местности вимондхам, норфолк, Англия. Её отца звали John Freeman. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Chamberlin Sr в 1615 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 28 января 1679 г. в местности хулл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности хулл, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Grace Freeman nació en 1595, en Wymondham, Norfolk, Inglaterra como hija de John Freeman. Se casó con Henry Chamberlin Sr en 1615, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 28 de enero de 1679, en Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Hull, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
